content.full_text:
Fatn Polrrrcel PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
February 3,2006
Anthony Watson
1432E,. Sunview Drive
Orange, CA 92865
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-002
Dear Mr. Watson:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'.Act").I
QUESTIONS
1. If a candidate for State Senate or Assembly accepts the voluntary expenditure
limits set forth in section 85400, who pays for a candidate statement (not to
exceed 250 words) thatmay be placed in the sample ballot pamphlet?
2. If a candidate for State Senate or Assembly has to pay for the right to place
this candidate statement in the sample ballot pamphlet, how much will this
cost?
CONCLUSIONS
1. The cost associated with purchasing space to put this statement in the voter
information portion of the sample ballot pamphlet is incurred by the
candidate.
2. The total cost for placing this statement in the voter information portion of the
sample ballot pamphlet will vary depending on the county, and is not
addressed by the Act. Each county sets its own fees based on the many
factors involved in preparing the sample ballot.
I Governm"rt Code sections 8 t 000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
qgl.E


File No. A-06-002
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are considering running for the 60e Assembly District seat. You inquire
about the cost ofplacing a statement on the ballot pamphlet.
ANALYSIS
Before any contributions are solicited or received, and before any campaign
expenditures are made from personal funds, candidates for statewide elective office must
file a Candidate Intention Statement-Form 501. (Sections 85200 and B52OL ) On Form
501, a candidate must state whether he or she accepts orrejects the voluntary expenditure
ceiling. Senate and Assembly Candidates who acceptthe ceiling are designated in the
voter information portion of the sample ballot and may purchase space to place there a
statement that does not exceed 250 words in length. The candidate must "hoor" to accept
or reject the expenditure ceilings for both the pimary and general elections at the time of
filing Form 501.
Section 85601 (c) states:
"A candidate for State Senate or Assembly who accepts the voluntary
expenditure limits set forth in Section 85400 may purchase the space to place
a statement in the voter information portion of the sample ballot that does not
exceed 250 words."
The statute is clear that the candidate, should he or she choose to place a
statement in the sample ballot pamphlet, is responsible for assuming thosi costs.
Lastly, the Act does not provide for a uniform fee for the cost ofplacing a
candidate statement in the sample ballot pamphlet. As this issue is not addressid by the
Act, you will have to check with the county or counties that are encompassed by the
District seat for which you intend to be a candidate in order to receive in exact cost for
placing this statement in the sample ballot pamphlet.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Christopher J. Espinosa
Executive Fellow, Legal Division
